title: NIS2 Secure System Acquisition and Development Lifecycle

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(e) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) (Security in acquisition, development and maintenance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.15: NIS2 Secure System Acquisition and Development Lifecycle.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.15' running on index devops and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=devops` `sourcetype=github:audit` (action, repo, actor) and `sourcetype=gitlab:audit` via HEC/webhooks; `index=devops` `sourcetype=jenkins:build` for on-prem pipelines; `index=sast` `sourcetype=sonarqube:issues` (severity, project, key); Splunk Add-on for GitHub ([Splunkbase 5596](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5596)) REST modular inputs hitting `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions` pulls complement webhook feeds.
app: Splunk Add-on for GitHub ([Splunkbase 5596](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5596)), Splunk Add-on for Jira ([Splunkbase 1438](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1438)) for change linkage, generic **HTTP Event Collector** for GitLab/Jenkins JSON.
spl:
index=devops sourcetype IN ("github:webhook","gitlab:pipeline","jenkins:build") earliest=-30d
| eval has_security_scan=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)sast|sca|snyk|sonar|trivy|semgrep|security.scan|code.scan"), 1, 0)
| eval deployment=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)deploy|release|prod|production"), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_builds, sum(has_security_scan) as scanned_builds, sum(deployment) as deployments by repository
| eval scan_coverage=round(100*scanned_builds/total_builds, 1)
| where scan_coverage < 80 OR (deployments > 0 AND scanned_builds = 0)
| sort scan_coverage
| table repository, total_builds, scanned_builds, scan_coverage, deployments
description: Measures, per GitHub/GitLab/Jenkins repository, the share of pipeline runs in the last 30 days that show evidence of a security scan stage and calls out repos that still shipped production keywords without any scanning hits.
value: Executives testing NIS2 development controls get a plain scoreboard of which product teams skip automated checks before code hits production, so security champions know where to spend coaching hours before an audit interview.
implementation: (1) Enable GitHub audit streaming or webhook posts into `index=devops` with `sourcetype=github:webhook`. (2) Mirror GitLab pipeline JSON through HEC token on port **8088** if cloud SaaS cannot install the TA directly. (3) Tag production branches so `deployment` regex matches real releases, not developer chatter. (4) Pair SonarQube issues (`index=sast`) in a sister dashboard for depth. (5) Escalate repos below `80%` coverage to engineering leads in Jira (1438).
visualization: Table of failing repositories, bar chart of scan_coverage, single value fleet average, line trend of coverage over 90 days.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Mono-repo with scan jobs in a different workflow file** — Distinguish: `_raw` never mentions SAST keywords but Sonar runs nightly outside CI. Suppress: lookup `repo_scan_exceptions.csv` documenting alternate job names.
• **Blue/green deploy phrases in logs without customer impact** — Distinguish: developers type "prod-like" in feature branches. Suppress: restrict `deployment=1` to default branch via `where match(branch,"^(main|master|release/)`)` after field extract.
• **Security scanning handled only in release trains quarterly** — Distinguish: policy allows quarterly bulk scans vs per-commit. Suppress: time-bound exception register `sdlc_policy_exceptions.csv` with repo and effective_through.
• **Jenkins shared library hides scan stage text** — Distinguish: `_raw` lacks keywords though library runs scans. Suppress: enrich with `lookup repo_has_external_sonar.csv` and OR that flag into `has_security_scan`.
• **High churn forks or student sandboxes** — Distinguish: repository tagged `classification=personal` in GitHub metadata. Suppress: `where classification!="personal"` after metadata join.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596) installed on heavy forwarder + search head so REST modular inputs and lookup definitions stay synchronized.
• HEC token (Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector) dedicated to CI JSON; index=`devops`, sourcetype forced in HEC ack channel.
• RBAC: pipeline visibility often contains secrets metadata — restrict `devops` index to engineering security roles only.
• Capacity: assume **2–5 KB** per pipeline event; large enterprises should set **30 day** retention hot tier then roll to frozen for cost control.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure GitHub Enterprise webhooks for `workflow_run` events pointed at your collector URL or enable the TA modular input hitting `api.github.com` every **300 seconds** for org-level audit logs.
For GitLab.com, use `log_level=info` pipeline webhooks because JSON includes job names needed for regex tuning.
Jenkins: install Splunk plug-in or file monitor `jenkins.log` via UF; map to `sourcetype=jenkins:build` with **DATETIME_CONFIG** tuned for ISO8601.
Optional Sonar hook: route issues to `index=sast` `sourcetype=sonarqube:issues` for deeper findings; link keys via `repository` name.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Execute SPL across **30 days** (`earliest=-30d@d`). Save as `UC-22.2.15_pipeline_scan_coverage`. Schedule weekly for management reporting; daily for regulated entities with fast release cadence.
Parameterize thresholds via macro `%(nis2_scan_coverage_threshold)` default **80**.
Add post-processing to join ServiceNow change (`snow:change`) if you need proof that deployments tied to CAB approvals.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick repository with known GitHub Action `security/snyk` job; confirm `_raw` contains keyword and `has_security_scan=1` count increments.
Run `| stats sum(deployment) by repository` and reconcile with GitHub Releases page counts.
Validate false repo list against internal CMDB `owner_email` to ensure notifications reach correct directorate.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard layout: top single value for fleet coverage, middle heatmap repo x week, bottom table sorted ascending on scan_coverage. Enable drilldown to raw events filtered on repository.
Integrate Jira Automation: webhook triggered when saved search yields new repo row.
Archive weekly CSV to `index=audit_evidence` with `host=splunk-sh` source=`nis2_sdlc_kpi`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Zero events** — HEC token wrong index or ACK disabled; check `splunkd.log` for `HttpInputHandler` errors.
• **Regex never matches** — scanners renamed ("CodeQL" only) — expand alternation in `match()`.
• **coverage stuck at 100 but audits fail** — `total_builds` tiny sample; widen to **90 days** window.
• **Performance slow** — add `summariesonly` not applicable to raw; instead schedule summary index generation nightly via `collect`.
• **Duplicate pipeline rows** — dedup `build_url` field before stats using `| dedup build_url`.
grandmaExplanation: We check whether the automatic safety checks ran before new software left the factory floor, so risky changes are caught while they are still easy to undo instead of after customers are affected.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: Weekly saved search `UC-22.2.15`, exported KPI workbook, and companion panel in the secure engineering dashboard referenced in the NIS2 evidence register.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture pushes 12 synthetic `jenkins:build` events for `repo-a` with zero `_raw` matches for security tooling words but two events mentioning `deploy to production`; the search returns `repo-a` with `scan_coverage` below 80 and deployments greater than zero.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fixture sends only events for `repo-b` where each build string includes `sonar` and none mention production deploys; the saved search output is empty for `repo-b` even though build volume is high.
